title: Instrument sri-lankais
text_preview: Minister of Foreign Affairs dri Lanka 18th November, 1982 Excellency, I have the honour to refer to your letter dated 28th July, 1982 and my reply thereto dated 8th September, 1982 concerning the Convention between the Government of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka and the Government of the Republio of France for the Avoidance of Double Taxation and the Prevention of Fiscal Evasion with respect to Taxe…

pages.text:
Minister of Foreign Affairs

dri Lanka

18th November, 1982

Excellency,

I have the honour to refer to your letter dated 28th July, 1982 and my reply thereto dated 8th September, 1982 concerning the Convention between the Government of the Democratic Socialist

Republic of Sri Lanka and the Government of the

Republio of France for the Avoidance of Double

Taxation and the Prevention of Fiscal Evasion with respect to Taxes on Income and Capital.

In accordance with Article XXVII thereof,

I am pleased to inform Your Excellency that the steps required by Sri Lanka Law to bring the above-mentioned Convention into force have been complied with.

Please accept, Excellency, the assurances of my highest consideration.

Honourable Mr Claude Cheysson,

Minister for External Relations,

Republic of France.
